channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Incoming Missouri state lawmaker introduces bill to give $1K to anyone who turns in illegal migrants
item.description: An incoming Republican Missouri state lawmaker proposed legislation to offer $1,000 to anyone who turns in illegal migrants to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five ways Trump could dismantle Biden's climate agenda
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to reverse several of President Biden's climate-related policies when he takes office in January,
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats say they'll avoid election challenges on Jan. 6
item.description: House Democrats say that they’ll skip the election protests they’ve staged on Jan. 6 in past presidential cycles, four years after supporters of President-elect Trump stormed the Capitol in an attempt to interrupt the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration announces $988M in aid to Ukraine on same day Trump meets with Zelenskyy in Paris
item.description: The Biden administration announced $988 million in aid to Ukraine after the president promised to give as much aid to the country as possible before President-elect Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Clintons urge voters agitated by today's politics to remain involved in public service
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Voters dejected by the presidential election results need to find a way to give back and remain involved, Bill and Hillary Rodham Clinton said Saturday as they celebrated the 20th anniversary of the Clinton presidential library.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress eyes 14.5% pay hike for junior troops, limits on transgender treatment in $895B defense bill
item.description: House and Senate negotiators have come to an agreement on how to spend $895 billion allocated federal dollars for U.S. national security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social media reacts to Trump 'dominating world leaders' with Macron handshake during meeting in France
item.description: President-elect Trump's handshake with French President Emmanuel Macron during a trip to France Saturday has gone viral on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Conservative group compiles list of 'woke' senior officers they want Pete Hegseth to fire
item.description: A conservative research group has compiled a list of 20 “woke" senior officers they want Pete Hegseth to fire should he be confirmed to lead the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Speaker’s Lobby: Thermonuclear Fracas
item.description: Despite the intent to find solutions following the assassination attempt of President-elect Trump back in July, bipartisan lawmakers got into a heated exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says US should stay out of fighting in Syria as opposition forces gain
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says the U.S. should avoid engaging militarily in Syria, where an opposition offensive is threatening the rule of the country's Russian- and Iran-allied leader.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The DNC chair candidates discuss Joe Biden, Kamala Harris and winning Latino voters
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — Four people are running to be the next Democratic National Committee chair, looking to take on the task of reinvigorating a party demoralized by a second loss to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dakotas poised for newfound political prominence with Senate, Trump Cabinet leaders picked from heartland
item.description: The Dakotas and Great Plains states are primed to be more geopolitically important than ever in the new congressional and presidential terms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Election Day has long passed. In some states, legislatures are working to undermine the results
item.description: Voters in some parts of the country are discovering that having their say at the ballot box is not necessarily the final word, even though this year's election was over a month ago.
item.dc:creator: Gary Fields, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: On pardons, Biden weighs whether to flex presidential powers in broad new ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden faces a stark choice as he contemplates broad preemptive pardons to protect aides and allies from potential retribution by Donald Trump: Does he hew to the institutional norms he’s spent decades defending or flex the powers of the presidency in untested ways? Th
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Melania Trump says heading to the White House for the second term is much different than the first
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Soon-to-be first lady Melania Trump said Friday that preparing to enter the White House for the second time looks a lot different from the first go-around. Now, she and her husband know what to expect.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Anthony Salisbury as deputy Homeland Security advisor
item.description: Anthony Salisbury is currently a special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) in Miami.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic rep pushes Biden to issue blanket pardon to those Patel could target
item.description: He noted that law enforcement officers, military personnel and others are at risk of extreme legal battles.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth slams 'manufactured media takedown' of him in WSJ op-ed
item.description: Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump's pick to lead the Defense Department, condemned the press for what he described as a "manufactured media takedown" of him. "The press is peddling anonymous story after anonymous story, all meant to smear me and tear me down.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho 'abortion trafficking' law will be enforced in part, court rules
item.description: Idaho can enforce a first-of-its-kind "abortion trafficking" law, a federal court ruled, making it illegal for anyone who transports a minor across state lines to get an abortion without parental consent.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son sparks calls for granting more clemencies
item.description: President Biden’s surprise pardon of his son Hunter has prompted calls for the outgoing commander in chief to grant clemency to other Americans before he hands over the White House to President-elect Trump next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are bringing Trump’s DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: The leaders of the so-called “Department of Government Efficiency” are calling for large-scale layoffs of federal workers and the elimination of some federal agencies during Trump's second term.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Steven Horsford to hand off role as chair of Congressional Black Caucus
item.description: Nevada Congressman Steven Horsford will no longer be the chair of the Congressional Black Caucus for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis City Council member Emily Koski joins mayor’s race
item.description: She joins Minnesota DFL Sen. Omar Fateh and the Rev. DeWayne Davis of Plymouth Congregational Church in challenging Mayor Jacob Frey, who’s running for reelection.
item.dc:creator: Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota budget update shows $1.1B less than prior forecast
item.description: The economic report released Wednesday morning by state budget officials projects a downturn, with only a $616 million cushion expected by the end of the next budget period. The new data will guide lawmakers when budget deliberations begin in January.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez first person to hit a million followers on Bluesky
item.description: The platform’s features are based off of Twitter, and they include the ability to message fellow users as well as a discover and feed tab.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
